notes.text: Neo feels disconnected from reality, living a double life as Thomas Anderson by day and hacker by night.
A talented but restless outsider searching for truth.
Neo receives strange signals about the Matrix and is contacted by Morpheus's network.
She initiates Neo's path by confirming that his world is not what it seems.
Neo hesitates when asked to escape agents through the office window, choosing apparent safety.
Fear of the unknown delays transformation.
Morpheus offers Neo the red pill or blue pill, framing the real stakes.
Guides Neo with conviction and belief in his potential.
Neo chooses the red pill and wakes in the real world, leaving his old identity behind.
Awakening has a cost: truth is harsher than illusion.
Training simulations, dojo fight, jump program, and early confrontations with danger test Neo's mindset.
An ally turned traitor who rejects the pain of reality.
The team visits the Oracle; hints about sacrifice and destiny increase pressure before the main conflict.
Offers ambiguous guidance that forces Neo to choose who to become.
Morpheus is captured; Neo chooses to return to the Matrix and face Agents to rescue him.
The hero earns identity through action, not prophecy.
Neo and Trinity free Morpheus in a high-risk rescue mission and survive impossible odds.
Critical partner in the rescue and emotional anchor for Neo.
Neo races to reach the exit while Agent Smith pursues him relentlessly.
Embodies systemic control and elimination of anomaly.
Neo is shot, dies, and returns to life with full awareness of his power in the Matrix.
Rebirth completes the internal transformation into The One.
Neo defeats Smith's immediate threat and sends a message promising liberation from the Matrix.
The gift returned is possibility: others can wake up too.
